title: Apply for citizenship: Urgent processing - Canada.ca
content:
## Apply for citizenship: Urgent processing
The current processing time for citizenship is We couldn’t load the data. Try again . If your situation is urgent and you need your citizenship sooner, you may apply for urgent processing.
We can only process citizenship applications urgently in exceptional cases , for example if you
- need Canadian citizenship to apply for a job avoid losing your job
- avoid losing your job
- need to travel because of death or serious illness in your family and you can’t get a passport
        in your current nationality
- receive a successful Federal Court decision on an appeal on a previous application for citizenship
We check every request to make sure we can process it urgently.
- Even if your application qualifies as an exceptional case, we may not be able to process your request urgently.
- If we process your request urgently, we may not be able to do it in time to help your situation.
- If your application doesn’t qualify as an exceptional case, we’ll process it normally.

## If you haven’t sent us your application:
- Download and print the application package you need
- Complete the application answer all the questions
- answer all the questions
- Write an explanation of why you need urgent processing include any documents that will support your explanation
- include any documents that will support your explanation
- In your envelope, include your: application explanation photographs fee payment required documents
- Write “ Request Urgent Processing – Grant of Citizenship ” in large, dark letters on the envelope
- Mail your application to the address in the instruction guide

## If you’ve already sent your application and now need urgent processing
Fill out the web form explaining why you need urgent processing and include:
- your full name, date of birth, email address and phone number
- your unique client identifier (UCI) or application number
- any documents that support your explanation accepted formats: PDF, JPEG, TIFF, DOC, DOCX file size Each file must be 2 MB or less. If you’re sending multiple files, the total size must be 3.5 MB or less. Find out how to reduce file size .
- accepted formats: PDF, JPEG, TIFF, DOC, DOCX
- file size Each file must be 2 MB or less. If you’re sending multiple files, the total size must be 3.5 MB or less. Find out how to reduce file size .
- Each file must be 2 MB or less.
- If you’re sending multiple files, the total size must be 3.5 MB or less.
- Find out how to reduce file size .

## Top questions about Canadian citizenship
- What are the requirements for becoming a Canadian citizen?
- I already have a citizenship application in process. How will the 2017 changes to the citizenship legislation affect my application?
- Do I become a Canadian when I marry a Canadian?
- How much does it cost to apply for Canadian citizenship?
- I am a citizen of another country. Will I lose that citizenship if I become a Canadian?
